objective: Design and implement Phase 6 (Learning + Memory System) within AI Growth OS while maintaining strict architectural layering, deterministic behavior, and backward compatibility.
decisions: Memory retention policy defined as hybrid (short-term Redis with TTL 3600 seconds + long-term SQLite persistence)
Long-term promotion trigger defined as repeated events ≥ 3 times or high-impact events (failure/success spike)
Eviction strategy defined as TTL-based for short-term and score-based pruning (future Phase 7)
Learning signal defined as composite model with weighted inputs (execution outcome 0.5, system metrics 0.3, planner efficiency 0.2)
Learning score threshold defined as 0.7 for promotion to long-term memory
Access control defined with strict layering: orchestrator (read/write via wrapper), agents (read + report only), planner (read-only), wrappers and engine (full access)
No direct infra access allowed outside wrapper layer
Agents must not write memory directly and must use standardized report_outcome()
Planner allowed to be memory-aware but not write to memory
Backward compatibility enforced (no endpoint or contract breaking)
System must remain deterministic and avoid uncontrolled learning behavior
Phase 6 implemented as minimal viable learning system to enable transition from L2 (reactive) to L3 (adaptive)

built_components.details: Handles event storage, retrieval, and promotion to long-term memory
Computes weighted score and triggers long-term promotion
Controls read/write access between system layers and memory
Standardizes agent outcome reporting and triggers learning updates
Persistent storage for long-term memory (table: long_term_memory)
Reads memory context and injects into execution context
Planner can access memory context for adaptive planning (placeholder)

inputs: User-defined system constraints for AI Growth OS (multi-phase architecture, strict layering, deterministic execution)
Phase completion state (Phase 0–5 completed, system stable at L2)
User confirmation to proceed with Phase 6
User inability to define retention, signal, and access leading to assistant-driven recommendations
Final user confirmation of Phase 6 configuration
outputs: Defined Phase 6 architecture aligned with existing system
Finalized configuration for memory retention, learning signals, and access scope
Six complete implementation files (db.py, memory_store.py, memory_engine.py, learning_engine.py, memory_adapter.py, feedback_handler.py)
Integration instructions for orchestrator, agents, and planner
Validation steps using Redis and SQLite
notes: Core Phase 6 modules and architecture are fully designed and implemented. However, execution is partial because orchestrator memory injection, agent feedback integration, and planner adaptive logic require manual wiring into existing system. Advanced learning features (adaptive thresholds, pruning, pattern recognition) are deferred to Phase 7.
